title: Headline yield vs pocket yield
intro: Distribution yield is not the income you keep.
prompt: An InvIT advertises a 12% distribution yield, but 40% of the payout is return of capital. Estimate the approximate 'income' portion of the yield.
debrief: Return of capital is your own money handed back, not earnings. 12% × (1 − 0.40) ≈ 7.2% is the income-like part — and even that ignores ageing assets and unit-price risk.
